codeReview.summary: The unified search API is well-implemented with clean code, proper error handling, and comprehensive tests. The implementation covers all required functionality: cross-model search (Product by name, Event by title/team1/team2/artistName), case-insensitive matching via JS toLowerCase(), trending items for empty/whitespace queries (prioritized by isBestValue/isSellingFast), and edge case handling (special chars, long strings, empty results, DB errors). The middleware was correctly updated to make the route public. The implementation follows existing codebase patterns (client-side filtering for SQLite compatibility, Prisma singleton usage, NextRequest/NextResponse patterns). 13 comprehensive unit tests cover all expectedBehavior scenarios. Two non-blocking observations noted below.

issues.description: Scalability: For non-empty queries, the endpoint fetches ALL products and ALL events from the database, then filters in JavaScript. This mirrors the existing pattern in /api/products and /api/events routes (necessary because SQLite doesn't support Prisma's case-insensitive mode), so it's consistent. However, as the dataset grows, this will become a performance bottleneck. Consider adding a `take` limit (e.g., 50) to the search results path, or documenting this as a known limitation for future migration to a DB with native case-insensitive search (e.g., PostgreSQL with mode: 'insensitive').

Error response shape diverges from the success shape. On error, returns { error: 'Failed to search' } (status 500) instead of { products: [], events: [], error: '...' }. Consumers must handle two different response shapes. This matches the existing pattern in /api/products and /api/events routes, so it's consistent with codebase conventions, but worth noting for API consumers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: When adding a new public API route, the developer must also update middleware.ts config.matcher to exclude it from auth protection. The worker initially forgot this, discovered the issue via manual curl testing (got redirected to /login), and had to make a separate commit. AGENTS.md does not document this middleware exclusion requirement for public API routes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows the worker tested with curl, got redirected to login page ('/login?callbackUrl=%2Fapi%2Fsearch%3Fq%3Djersey'), then had to edit middleware.ts and make a second commit (68685843). middleware.ts config.matcher line 112 needed updating.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md 'API Patterns > Public routes' section lists /api/products, /api/events, /api/vip-tiers but does not include the newly added /api/search. This list should be kept in sync as new public routes are added.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md lists 'Public routes: /api/products, /api/events, /api/vip-tiers' but /api/search is now also a public route per middleware.ts config.matcher.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The platform-worker skill procedure (step 5) requires interactive verification with agent-browser on mobile viewport. The worker skipped this step entirely — no agent-browser invocation appears in the transcript skeleton. The handoff reports skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true, but this is inaccurate since interactive verification was not performed. The skill may need clarification that agent-browser verification is required only for UI features, not pure API features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows no Skill invocation for 'agent-browser'. Handoff has no 'interactiveChecks' array. The worker did perform manual curl testing, which is adequate for an API-only feature, but the skill doesn't distinguish between API and UI features.

summary: PASS. The unified search API implementation is solid and covers all feature requirements. Two commits: 134249bc (API route + tests) and 68685843 (middleware fix for public access). The code follows existing codebase patterns, all 13 tests are comprehensive and well-structured, and edge cases are handled. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) fetching all records for in-memory filtering (consistent with existing SQLite workaround pattern), (2) error response shape inconsistency (also consistent with existing routes). Three shared state observations: middleware exclusion convention undocumented in AGENTS.md, architecture.md public routes list needs updating, and skill procedure for agent-browser doesn't distinguish API vs UI features.
